subject: GRB 230806A: Fermi GBM Final Localization
submitter: Peter Veres at University of Alabama in Huntsville <veresp@gmail.com>
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

"At 04:02:32.37 UT on 06 August 2023, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB 230806A (trigger 712987357/230806168).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 63.12, Dec = -7.33 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 4h 12m, -7d 19'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 18.10 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 64 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230806168/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn230806168.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230806168/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn230806168.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230806168/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn230806168.gif"
